Freshly purified CD8+ T cells were cultured in RPMI-1640 culture medium supplemented with 10% heat-inactivated FBS, 1% penicillin/streptomycin, and 10 ng/mL recombinant human IL-2 (PeproTech, USA) on tissue culture plates precoated with 3.5 µg/mL human anti-CD3 (BioLegend, USA) and 1 µg/mL anti-CD28 (BioLegend) antibodies. Fresh media containing IL-2 were replenished every 2 days, and the cells were maintained in culture for a period of 5 to 7 days.

sec.title: CD8+ T-cell-mediated tumor cell killing assay
sec.p: Pretransfected BXPC-3 or ASPC-1 cells (5 × 10⁴ cells/well) were plated in 24-well plates and pretreated with trametinib (MCE, HY-10999, 100 nM) or vehicle control (DMSO ≤ 0.1%) for 24 h. Activated CD8+ T cells (5 × 10⁵ cells/well) were cocultured with target cells at a 10:1 effector: target (E: T) ratio in complete RPMI-1640 medium. The experimental groups were supplemented with an anti-human PD-1 mAb (pembrolizumab biosimilar, Bio X Cell, BE0189, 20 µg/mL), whereas the control groups received isotype-matched human IgG4 (Bio X Cell, CP148, 20 µg/mL). After 16 h of incubation under standardized conditions (37 °C, 5% CO₂), the cell culture supernatants were harvested and clarified by centrifugation (400×g, 5 min, 4 °C). Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) release was measured using the LDH Cytotoxicity Assay Kit (Beyotime, C0016) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. The absorbance at 490 was determined nm using a microplate reader.

sec.title: PLA
sec.p: According to the manufacturer’s instructions, PLA was performed with some modifications using the Duolink in situ kit. PDAC cells were harvested after 4 days of culture and then fixed with 2% formaldehyde (PFA) for 10 min at room temperature. The fixed cells were stained with mouse anti-HSC70 and rabbit anti-MEK1 antibodies for 1 h at room temperature. After staining, the cells were washed and then incubated with secondary mouse PLUS and rabbit secondary antibody MINUS for 30 min at room temperature. The cells were subsequently washed with TBS (0.01 M sodium phosphate, 0.15 M NaCl) containing 0.5% bovine serum albumin (BSA), followed by a 30-minute ligation reaction at 37 °C and a 100-minute amplification step at 37 °C. Cells were washed again with TBS containing 0.5% BSA. Observations were made under a confocal microscope.

sec.title: Mouse experiments
sec.p: Six- to eight-week-old nude and wild-type C57BL/6 mice were procured from Anbiorui Biochemistry Laboratory (Fuzhou, China). Age-matched littermates of both sexes were housed under specific pathogen-free (SPF) conditions at Anbiorui BD Laboratory (Fuzhou, China). The cohort sizes for individual experiments are detailed in the corresponding figure legends. All procedures strictly adhered to the National Institutes of Health (NIH) Guidelines for the Care and Use of Laboratory Animals and were approved by the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee of Fujian Provincial Hospital (Protocol No. IACUC-FPH-SL-20240530[0312]). Animals were prospectively randomized into experimental groups by an independent investigator, with strict segregation maintained between the treatment and control groups. Humane endpoints were defined on the basis of the following institutional ethical standards: (1) tumor burden ≤ 2.0 cm in maximal diameter; (2) impairment of essential functions (feeding, elimination, or ambulation); or (3) severe deterioration of body condition. Euthanasia was performed via cervical dislocation under anesthesia. The tumor dimensions were rigorously monitored to maintain a maximal diameter < 2 cm throughout the study period. The experimental protocols and termination criteria align with the ARRIVE guidelines 2.0 for preclinical studies.

For the subcutaneous model, six- to eight-week-old nude mice received a subcutaneous injection of 1 × 106 genetically modified ASPC-1 (OE-SERPINB3 or control) or BXPC-3 (sh-SERPINB3 or control) cells. Tumors were harvested at three weeks post-injection for assessment of tumor growth. The treatment methods are described in the corresponding legends.

For the hepatic metastasis model, six- to eight-week-old nude mice received intrasplenic injections of 1 × 106 genetically modified ASPC-1 (OE-SERPINB3 or control) or BXPC-3 (sh-SERPINB3 or control) cells. Metastatic progression was assessed four weeks after the procedure.

For the orthotopic tumor model, six- to eight-week-old nude or C57BL/6 mice received intrapancreatic injections of 1 × 106 OE-SERPINB3 or control luciferase-expressing KPC or ASPC-1 cells; tumor growth was evaluated by bioluminescence imaging post-implantation, and the tumors were harvested for further analysis at the above-described end points. The treatment methods are described in the corresponding legends.

sec.title: Human tissues
sec.p: Human PDAC tissue samples were obtained from Fujian Provincial Hospital and Fujian Medical University Union Hospital. These samples were utilized for IHC, PDOs generation, mIFS, and scRNA-seq. Technical support for mIFS was provided by Wuhan Servicebio Technology Co., LTD. The study protocol was approved by the Ethics Committee of Fujian Provincial Hospital (IRB No. K2024-06–027).

sec.title: ScRNA-seq
sec.p: Four pancreatic cancer tissue samples sensitive to cuproptosis and four pancreatic cancer tissue samples resistant to cuproptosis were collected for analysis. The tissues were enzymatically digested at 37 °C for 30 minutes in DMEM/F12 medium supplemented with 0.25% collagenase II and IV and 0.05% DNase I. Following digestion, the cells were counted, and 1.5 × 104 primary cancer cells from each sample were loaded onto the 10X Genomics Chromium platform. Single-cell libraries were prepared using the Single-cell 3’ v2 Reagent Kit (10X Genomics) according to the manufacturer’s protocol. Gel bead-in-emulsion (GEM) generation, barcoding, GEM-RT purification, cDNA amplification, and library preparation were performed as per standard procedures. Library quantification was performed using a Qubit fluorometer, and pooled libraries were sequenced on an Illumina NovaSeq 6000 system with 150 bp paired-end reads. The raw sequencing data were aligned to the human reference genome (mm10-3.0.0) using CellRanger (version 3.1.0). External validation datasets were obtained from publicly available repositories (CRA001160, GSE154778, GSE155698, GSE202740, and GSE212966). Downstream analyses were conducted in R (version 4.0.3) using the Seurat package (version 2.2). Quality control filtering excluded genes expressed in fewer than two cells and retained cells expressing more than 200 genes, whose mitochondrial gene content was less than 10%. The coefficient of variation for genes was calculated using Seurat. Dimensionality reduction was performed via principal component analysis (PCA) on the top 2000 most variable genes. A k-nearest neighbor graph was constructed on the basis of Euclidean distances in the space of the first 10 principal components. Cell clustering was achieved using the Louvain modularity optimization algorithm, and the results were visualized via uniform manifold approximation and projection (UMAP). Hemoglobin-expressing cells were excluded from further analysis. Differential gene expression analysis for each cluster was performed using the ‘bimod’ test implemented in Seurat’s FindMarkers function. Marker genes were defined as those with a log2-fold change > 0.585 and p < 0.05. The cell clusters were annotated using canonical markers from the CellMarker-2.082 database. Gene Ontology (GO) term enrichment analysis was conducted, with significantly enriched terms selected on the basis of a false discovery rate (FDR) threshold of ≤ 0.05. The scRNA-seq experiments were supported by Singleron Biotechnologies Co., Ltd. (Nanjing, China).

sec.title: Statistical analysis
sec.p: All statistical analyses were conducted using GraphPad Prism version 9.1.2. Data are expressed as mean ± SD throughout the study unless otherwise indicated. For comparisons between two groups, continuous variables were analyzed using two-tailed Student’s t-test for normally distributed data or Mann-Whitney U test for nonparametric distributions. Categorical variables were assessed with two-tailed Pearson’s χ² test or Fisher’s exact test, depending on expected frequency counts. A two-way ANOVA was used for multiple comparisons, followed by post hoc analysis. Survival analyses were performed by constructing Kaplan-Meier curves, with intergroup differences evaluated using log-rank tests. A probability threshold of P < 0.05 was predefined as statistically significant for all comparisons.

All the animal experiments in this study were conducted in the Animal Experimental Center in Anburui Biotechnology Co., Ltd. (Fuzhou, China). All diagrams in this study were created with BioRender.com.

Huo-Juan Zhu and Wan-Wan Wang synthesized and verified the nanomaterial. Yi Huang, Zu-Wei Wang, Long Huang, and Yi-Feng Tian provided technical help. All authors reviewed the manuscript.

notes.title: Data availability
notes.p: The datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.

notes.title: Ethics approval and consent to participate
notes.p: Human PDAC tissues obtained from Fujian Provincial Hospital and Fujian Medical University Union Hospital were utilized for IHC analysis, PDOs establishment, mIFS, and scRNA-seq investigations. Technical execution of mIFS was conducted by Wuhan Servicebio Technology Co., Ltd. (Wuhan, China). This study protocol received ethical approval from the Institutional Review Board of Fujian Provincial Hospital (IRB No. K2024-06-027), with written informed consent obtained from all participating donors. Animal experimental procedures were reviewed and approved by the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee of Fujian Provincial Hospital (Protocol No. IACUC-FPH-SL-20240530[0312]) and strictly adhered to the National Institutes of Health (NIH) Guidelines for the Humane Use and Care of Laboratory Animals.

notes.title: Consent for publication
notes.p: The authors understand that the text and any pictures or videos published in the article will be freely available on the internet and may be seen by the general public. The pictures, videos and text may also appear on other websites or in print, may be translated into other languages or used for commercial purposes. The authors have been offered the opportunity to read the manuscript.

notes.title: Competing interests
notes.p: The authors declare no competing interests.
